skill.name: seedance-v2
skill.description: Generate cinematic short-form video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro on RunComfy. Documents Seedance 2.0 Pro's strengths (multi-modal references — up to 9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio — synchronized in-pass audio with natural lip-sync, cinematic motion refinement), the 4–15s duration schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Wan 2.7 / Kling instead. Calls `runcomfy run bytedance/seedance-v2/pro` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "seedance", "seedance 2", "seedance v2", "seedance pro", "bytedance video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro via RunComfy CLI with multi-modal references and native lip-sync
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 89 potential issues across external_commands, network, filesystem, and crypto patterns. After semantic evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backticks are documentation examples (not executable code), URLs point to legitimate RunComfy service endpoints, path references are documentation placeholders, and crypto/system references are in documentation context. The skill is a well-structured prompt template with clear security boundaries documented in the SKILL.md file.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: 67 backtick references detected in SKILL.md flagged as external_commands. These are documentation examples showing CLI syntax, not executable code. The skill is a prompt template that documents how to invoke the RunComfy CLI. No actual shell execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown documentation are standard syntax highlighting, not shell commands. The skill explicitly states prompts are transmitted as JSON without shell expansion.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs
low_findings.description: 10 hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com and model-api.runcomfy.net endpoints. These are legitimate service documentation URLs for the RunComfy API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to runcomfy.com domains - the legitimate service provider for this skill.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Patterns in Documentation
low_findings.description: References to <absolute/path> placeholders in CLI examples. These are documentation examples, not actual path operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The <absolute/path> syntax explicitly indicates placeholder text for user configuration, not actual file operations.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access Reference
low_findings.description: Reference to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json for token storage. This is documented configuration behavior, not malicious access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage in ~/.config is standard practice for CLI tools, documented with proper file permissions (mode 0600).

content.user_title: Generate Cinematic Video with Seedance 2.0 Pro
content.value_statement: Create professional short-form videos with natural lip-sync and multi-modal references. Seedance 2.0 Pro combines image, video, and audio references into coherent cinematic shots without separate TTS pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Generate 4–15 second cinematic videos with native lip-synced audio
Combine up to 9 reference images, 3 videos, and 3 audio files in a single prompt
Create character-consistent spokesperson and dialogue videos using image references
Produce ambient audio, SFX, and background music synchronized to the video
Control output with seed values for reproducible variant testing
limitations: Video duration capped at 4–15 seconds per generation
Maximum resolution of 720p on the playground variant
Reference videos and audio must be 2–15 seconds; audio files under 15MB
Lip-sync quality depends on prompt clarity; not guaranteed perfect in all scenarios

use_cases.title: Spokesperson Video Ads
use_cases.description: Create brand-consistent dialogue ads with character image references. The AI maintains facial identity across multiple takes while the prompt drives the evolving narrative.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and advertisers creating social media content

use_cases.title: Multilingual Brand Narratives
use_cases.description: Use image references to maintain brand identity across languages. The text prompt carries the translation while the reference image holds the visual consistency.
use_cases.target_user: Global brands managing localized video campaigns

use_cases.title: Cinematic Pre-Visualization
use_cases.description: Generate film previs with camera directions, motion cues, and audio ambience. Combine reference footage and audio to establish the visual tone before full production.
use_cases.target_user: Filmmakers and creative directors planning video productions

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Golden hour on a cafe terrace: a barista wipes the counter, then looks up and explains today's special in a friendly tone, natural lip-sync. Medium close-up, slow push-in; warm side light, soft bokeh, gentle cafe ambience and subtle film grain.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic video generation from a descriptive prompt

prompt_templates.title: Character-Consistent Lip-Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Same person as image 1 in a recording booth, leaning into the mic, says: "We just shipped the biggest update of the year." Calm conversational tone. Medium close-up, locked tripod, shallow DOF, warm key light from camera-left.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Modal Cinematic
prompt_templates.prompt: Subject from image 1 walks through the cafe from video 1, voice tone matches audio 1. Slow cinematic tracking shot, golden hour lighting, gentle bokeh background.
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining image, video, and audio references

prompt_templates.title: Brand Ad with Audio Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Brand ambassador explains the new product line with tone matching audio 1 reference. Wide establishing shot transitioning to close-up, clean retail lighting, confident professional delivery.
prompt_templates.scenario: Audio-guided voice tone with narrative progression

output_examples.input: Generate a 10-second video of a chef explaining today's special in a warm conversational tone. Use the provided headshot for character consistency.
output_examples.output: A 10-second MP4 with the chef from the reference image speaking naturally, with synchronized lip movement matching the described tone. Output resolution: 720p, 9:16 aspect ratio.

output_examples.input: Create a cinematic product reveal with ambient cafe ambience. Reference the product shot image and the voice tone from the audio sample.
output_examples.output: A cinematic product reveal video showing the referenced product in a cafe setting with the referenced voice tone for narration. Includes ambient background sounds and professional lighting.

best_practices: Use image references for stable identity (faces, costumes, brand marks) rather than describing them in text prompts
Put evolving narrative, actions, and camera directions in the text prompt while keeping character references in image_url
Match reference media aspect ratios to your intended output to avoid unwanted cropping
anti_patterns: Mixing radically different aesthetic references (watercolor art style with photorealistic images) causes model confusion
Describing stable visual identity verbally instead of using image_url references leads to character drift
Requesting clips longer than 15 seconds results in 422 errors; segment into multiple generation calls

faq.question: What video formats does Seedance 2.0 Pro support for output?
faq.answer: Seedance 2.0 Pro outputs MP4 videos with 720p resolution by default. You can choose between 480p and 720p, and select aspect ratios including 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1, and 21:9.

faq.question: How does lip-sync work in Seedance 2.0 Pro?
faq.answer: When generate_audio is true (default), the model generates synchronized speech with natural lip movement from your text prompt. You can describe the tone (warm, friendly, instructional) and the model produces matching audio with lip-sync in the video.

faq.question: Can I use my own reference audio for voice guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. You can provide up to 3 audio references via audio_url (2–15 seconds each, under 15MB). These guide the voice tone and style without locking the lip-sync to a specific recording.

faq.question: What happens if I provide conflicting style references?
faq.answer: Mixing incompatible aesthetic references (such as watercolor art with photorealistic photography) can confuse the model. For best results, use references that share a similar visual style or keep them in separate generations.

faq.question: How do I maintain character consistency across multiple clips?
faq.answer: Provide a character image reference in image_url for consistent identity. The model maintains the character's appearance across all generations using that reference image. Avoid trying to describe the character in text.

faq.question: What's the difference between Seedance 2.0 Pro and other video models like Wan or Kling?
faq.answer: Seedance 2.0 Pro excels at multi-modal references (combining images, videos, and audio in one prompt) and native lip-sync generation. Use HappyHorse 1.0 for highest quality, Wan 2.7 for audio-driven lip-sync from your own track, and Kling Video O1 for motion editing on existing footage.
